title: 1981-04-16_Pat_116.txt
key_claims: Dick contrasts the Antique Tragic (succumbing to fate/heimarmene) with the Modern Heroic (seizing your death and making it your own). Seizing fate transforms destiny from your master into your artifact.
Christ's vicarious atonement was not a passive defeat; it was a trick or ruse devised by Holy Wisdom (Hagia Sophia) to ensnare the demonic powers. The sacrificial lamb was bait swallowed by evil.
The "Heroic" entered the world meekly as this sacrificial Lamb, successfully tricking the archaic powers of fate.
By surviving 2-3-74 and writing *VALIS*, Dick believes he achieved his own heroic victory over death, devouring his fate as "Zagreus ate the Titans."
recurring_concepts: **The Parity of Defeat and Victory**: In the 180° reversed world, ostensible grand tragedy (the Empire's power) is actually cosmic defeat, while meek, invisible sacrifice is ultimate heroic victory.
reading_excerpt: Dick contrasts the Antique Tragic (succumbing to fate/heimarmene) with the Modern Heroic (seizing your death and making it your own). Seizing fate transforms destiny from your master into your artifact.
